message:
Bound memory during ingestion and merge duplicate conversations correctly

Streaming pipeline: add StreamingByteLimiter (Condvar-based) that caps
total text bytes across queued/in-flight batches, preventing oversized
single conversations from multiplying across the channel and blowing
the memory envelope. The limiter integrates into StreamingBatchSender,
StreamingProducerConfig, and the consumer loop, releasing reservations
after each batch is ingested. On consumer error the limiter is closed
to unblock any waiting producers.

Tantivy: add_messages now flushes documents in sub-batches of 512 docs
or 1 MB of text instead of accumulating the entire conversation in
memory before a single add_cass_documents call.

SQLite storage: replace the MAX(idx) cutoff with a HashSet of existing
message indices, correctly handling non-contiguous idx values (gaps).
New franken_insert_conversation_or_get_existing gracefully recovers
from UNIQUE constraint conflicts by reusing the existing conversation
row and appending only new messages. insert_conversations_batched now
tracks pending_conversation_ids and pending_message_indices across the
batch so duplicate external_ids within the same batch are merged into
one conversation. FTS inserts are flushed incrementally via
flush_pending_fts_entries (512 docs / 1 MB caps) rather than buffered
unboundedly.

Indexer: detect duplicate conversation keys before entering the
BEGIN CONCURRENT path and fall back to serial batched indexing, avoiding
write conflicts on the same row from concurrent connections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
